discussion_posts.name: wangshuoyuan2

discussion_posts.name: sttony
discussion_posts.content: robot start from (0, 0), but  (0, 0) is a obstacle.

discussion_posts.name: 21stCenturyLegend
discussion_posts.content:
This is for C++ audience.
Please note return type is mentioned int but if you are maintaining dp of int type you will always get Runtime error because of int range overflow.

So maintain dp of long long type.

Final position ( Bottom right ) will always have answer fit in integer type.

Then you may have question why we have to maintain dp of long long type. 

Reason: Consider the case when your answer at any position may overflow integer range but that position answer is not contributing to final answer because of obstacle.

discussion_posts.name: JustinAdams
discussion_posts.content: Examples could probably be a little more complex. The [[1,0]] test case was nasty.

discussion_posts.name: mingrui
discussion_posts.content:
Current test recognizes "0" as "right" output.
I think it should either be:
1. "[[1]]" is invalid input, since the robot can't start in obstacles.
2. "1" as right output, since the robot is already there at start.

discussion_posts.name: iamsuteerth
discussion_posts.content: Finally! I got a DP question right in a single go without taking any help!! \nFeeling a lot more confident to tackle more difficult problems all by myself!

discussion_posts.name: anwendeng
discussion_posts.content:
done before. Resubmit!
this is a DP problem! One more hint:
```
dp[i][j]=0 if obstacleGrid[i][j]==1
```
